title: rubus wahlbergii f. tenuifolius
intro: Rubus wahlbergii f. tenuifolius, also known as the Japanese raspberry, is a variant of the Japanese bramble plant, a species of raspberry native to East Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which are believed to have various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The leaves and stems of the Rubus wahlbergii f. tenuifolius plant are used in herbal preparations, often in the form of teas, infusions, and decoctions, to treat various health conditions. The plant's medicinal properties and health benefits make it a popular ingredient in traditional East Asian medicine, particularly in Chinese and Japanese herbalism.
